title: Subdomain Management Flow
description: Context provision, predicate creation, upsert, select, and max-subdomains enforcement in SubdomainES

steps: Get 'database' service, open DB_READ handle tagged 'subdomains'
YES → return new Eq({ key: 'owner', value: Context.get('user').id })
NO  → append Eq({ key: 'owner', value: user.id }) to options.predicate via .and()
Execute SQL: SELECT COUNT(id) AS subdomain_count FROM subdomains WHERE user_id = ?

flow_logic: upsert → [new entity?] → YES: _check_max_subdomains → [limit reached?] → YES: throw APIError | NO: upstream.upsert
select → [read-all permission?] → NO: filter by owner → upstream.select | YES: upstream.select unfiltered
key_relationships: SubdomainES delegates upsert/select to this.upstream (BaseES chain)
SubdomainES reads DB via DatabaseService (DB_READ mode)
SubdomainES uses Context for user/actor resolution
SubdomainES throws APIError on subdomain limit violation
important_notes: Enforcement only applies to INSERT (no old_entity). Updates bypass the limit check. Permission 'read-all-subdomains' grants unrestricted listing; otherwise results are owner-filtered. max_subdomains is user-level override, falling back to global config.
